summary: Completed the pre-release audit, documented scope/module-depth findings/risks/release recommendation, preserved prior low-risk dead-code suppression cleanup, and repaired the previous cargo-not-found verification failure by hardening release workflow cargo commands with $HOME/.cargo/bin.
acceptance_status: docs/release-audit.md exists and records audited scope, findings, fixes made, remaining risks, verification, and release recommendation.
Module-depth audit explicitly uses module, interface, seam, depth, leverage, and locality vocabulary.
Dirty/broken/dead/redundant/AI-slop audit completed; stale dead-code suppressions and cargo verification path issue were fixed, remaining risks documented.
No public CLI behavior, daemon IPC shape, JSON Issue Slice schema/semantics, worker result schema, repair result schema, or runner contract changed.
Final worktree is clean, committed, and full verification profile passed.

findings.description: Cargo-based quick/full verification commands now prepend $HOME/.cargo/bin, repairing the observed cargo-not-found verification failure in this worker shell.
Prior audit cleanup removed stale dead_code suppressions from used agent/gitutil items.
workflow::manager remains large; documented as release-acceptable but lower-locality internal risk for a future refactor.
Remaining lint allowances are documented as intentional test seam/private orchestration helper trade-offs; reshaping them would be a broad refactor.
ResultData.text and ResultData.usage are retained but not surfaced; documented as low-severity future cleanup.

assumptions: Audit scope stayed limited to committed repository code and release workflow assets; build outputs and transient runtime artifacts were excluded.
Cargo is available under $HOME/.cargo/bin or already on PATH; workflow commands now preserve PATH while prepending that common rustup location.
